helpful: Provisioned QP/petition/BIO text — carried nearly all the case-specific signal
Snapshot's full conference-distribution history — the relist count is the decisive covariate here
statpack relist-count cut (3+ relists -> 21.8% granted) as the quantitative anchor
fedcourts query rows exposing distribution_count and date_cert_granted for same-conference grants, which proved the petition was carried past the Term-end cleanup
gaps: statpack.md lacks the 'Segment base rate by salience band' table the prompt directs cert predictors to anchor on
The relist table's top bucket is '3+'; an extreme-relist bucket (e.g. 10+) would discriminate exactly the cases where the signal matters most
No provisioned text of the parties' supplemental briefs (the March/April 2026 'Benson' filings), which were material here; supremecourt.gov blocks direct PDF fetches (403)
notes: Forward cell in good shape: snapshot current to 7/14/2026, all three documents extracted. CourtListener MCP was configured but unneeded — web search resolved the intervening-decision questions (Benson, Wolford, 3d Cir en banc) faster than endpoint calls would have.
